ud8.7:1.2:
on one occasion the Blessed One was journeying along a road in the Kosalan country with Ven.
Nāgasamāla as his junior companion.
ud8.7:1.3: Ven. Nāgasamāla, while going along the road, saw a fork in the path.
ud8.7:1.4: On seeing it, he said to the Blessed One,
ud8.7:1.5: “That, lord Blessed One, is the route.
ud8.7:1.7: When this was said, the Blessed One said,
ud8.7:2.2: A third time, Ven. Nāgasamāla said to the Blessed One,
ud8.7:2.3: “That, lord Blessed One, is the route.
ud8.7:2.5: And for a third time, the Blessed One said,
ud8.7:2.8: Then Ven. Nāgasamāla, placing the Blessed One’s bowl & robes right there on the ground, left, saying,
ud8.7:2.9: “This, lord Blessed One, is the bowl & robes.”
ud8.7:3.1:
Then as Ven. Nāgasamāla was going along that route, thieves–jumping out in the middle of the road–
pummeled him with their fists & feet, broke his bowl, and ripped his outer robe to shreds.
ud8.7:3.2:
So Ven. Nāgasamāla–with his bowl broken, his outer robe ripped to shreds–
went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, bowed down to him and sat to one side.
ud8.7:3.3: As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud8.7:3.4:
“Just now, lord, as I was going along that route, thieves jumped out in the middle of the road,
pummeled me with their fists & feet, broke my bowl, and ripped my outer robe to shreds.”
ud8.7:4.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.7:5.2: mixed together with a person who doesn’t know, an attainer-of-wisdom,
ud8.7:5.3: on realizing that the person is evil, abandons him
ud8.7:5.4: as a milk-feeding heron, a bog.
